refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR is already focused on a single obligation: ensuring teleoperation communication quality meets a specified packet loss limit. It is solution-neutral, specifying what the system must achieve without prescribing how. Therefore, it is atomic and does not require decomposition.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the packet loss rate during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify it does not exceed the customer-defined limit.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.

candidate_dps: Implement a communication protocol with adaptive error correction and retransmission to maintain packet loss within limits
Design a dedicated communication channel with guaranteed quality of service (QoS) parameters to meet packet loss requirements
Incorporate real-time network monitoring and dynamic adjustment of transmission parameters to control packet loss
Use buffering and smoothing techniques at the receiver to compensate for packet loss and maintain teleoperation quality
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement a communication protocol with adaptive error correction and retransmission to maintain packet loss within limits

potential_sources: Tight integration between communication protocol and network monitoring modules
Dependency on specific network conditions or hardware capabilities
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on a logical communication protocol design that can be implemented independently of specific hardware or network technologies, supporting modularity and independence. However, care must be taken to avoid coupling with network monitoring or hardware-specific modules to maintain design independence.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions remain within agreed parameters to achieve packet loss targets
Risk that adaptive protocols may introduce latency affecting teleoperation responsiveness
Assumes availability of sufficient computational resources to implement adaptive error correction
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly separating network monitoring and communication protocol functions into distinct DPs or IMs to further reduce coupling and improve modularity.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the core communication and networking functionalities, including protocol implementation and adaptive error correction mechanisms.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Handles the physical and logical network connection over which packets are sent and received.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Sends status and performance data related to communication protocol operation.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as the communication protocol logically belongs here; minimal diffusion as error correction and retransmission are core networking functions.
Vehicle module uses communication but does not implement protocol logic; would cause significant diffusion if protocol implemented here.
Teleoperation depends on communication but protocol implementation here would mix concerns and increase coupling.
Safety Services may monitor communication but implementing protocol here is not cohesive and would cause high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and appropriate boundary to contain the communication protocol with adaptive error correction and retransmission, as it centralizes networking functions and minimizes diffusion across other modules.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on Network Link interface stability and performance.
If protocol requirements change to include safety-critical features, coordination with Safety Services may increase coupling.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP directly addresses the FR by specifying a communication protocol with adaptive error correction and retransmission to maintain packet loss within the customer-defined limit, satisfying the acceptance criteria of packet loss ≤ 1%.

diffusion_risks: Dependency on Network Link interface stability and performance may affect implementation.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module is highly cohesive for containing the communication protocol and adaptive error correction mechanisms, with minimal diffusion risk as these functions are centralized and not scattered across other modules.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through monitoring packet loss metrics and telemetry data emitted by the NGHE Solution module, ensuring compliance with the customer-defined packet loss limit.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the communication protocol DP to the NGHE Solution IM is clear and appropriate, with well-defined interfaces to Network Link and Telemetry modules supporting verification and operation.

recommended_revisions: Monitor and manage dependencies on the Network Link interface to mitigate risks related to its stability and performance.
Plan for coordination with Safety Services if future protocol requirements include safety-critical features to avoid increased coupling and diffusion.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully, indicating that the current functional requirement is adequately addressed by the design parameter and properly allocated to the implementation module. Verification feasibility and allocation clarity are confirmed, and diffusion risks are minimal and manageable. Given the high safety criticality, the current design includes appropriate containment and monitoring strategies. Therefore, no further decomposition into sub-FRs is necessary at this time.

assumptions: The communication protocol with adaptive error correction sufficiently meets the packet loss requirement of ≤ 1%.
The NGHE Solution module can reliably implement and contain the DP with minimal diffusion risk.
Dependencies on the Network Link interface can be effectively monitored and managed.
open_risks: Dependency on Network Link interface stability and performance may impact implementation robustness.
Potential future coordination needs with Safety Services if protocol requirements evolve to include safety-critical features.
evidence_needed: Telemetry data confirming packet loss remains within specified limits during operation.
Monitoring reports on Network Link interface performance and stability.
Documentation of interface definitions and module boundaries for NGHE Solution and related modules.
